qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public PDP notify callback permits unauthenticated loopback and internal SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a PDP notify SSRF through attacker-controlled notify URLs and a later http.Post sink, while the advisory is a database-error credential leak from raw err.Error() responses and DSN construction/redaction paths.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding is an outbound PDP notify SSRF, whereas the advisory is about raw database errors and connection-string secrets being returned to HTTP clients and logs.
evidence: The advisory JSON and PR #919 patch remediate credential exposure by replacing client-facing err.Error() responses in market/mk12/mk12_utils.go, market/mk20/http/http.go, and pdp/handlers.go, and by redacting DB errors in harmony/harmonydb.

public_pdp_notify_ssrf/vulnerability_report.json traces a different path: public PDP routing plus handlePiecePost storing attacker-controlled notify data and tasks/pdp/notify_task.go later calling http.Post(upload.NotifyURL, ...) without destination validation.

artifacts/component_family_checklist.md marks the PDP notify outbound-request family as the only accepted finding and explicitly leaves the mk20 family pruned, so the session did not produce a qualifying sensitive-data-exposure finding for the advisory paths.
